app.description: Cloudflare Tunnel creates a secure outbound-only connection from your server to Cloudflare's edge — no open inbound ports on your router required. On first run, the init container automatically creates a locally-managed tunnel via the Cloudflare API, adds wildcard DNS records for every DOMAINNAME_N in your .env, and configures cloudflared. No manual tunnel creation, DNS setup, or dashboard configuration needed.
app.descriptionShort: Cloudflare Tunnel — no open inbound ports

variables.name: CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_NAME
variables.description: Name for the tunnel in Cloudflare Zero Trust (e.g. homelab, plexy). Used only on first run.

dialog.title: API Token Scope Required
dialog.message:
Your cf_dns_api_token must have TWO permissions:

  Zone → DNS → Edit
  Account → Cloudflare Tunnel → Edit

If your existing token only has DNS:Edit, edit it at:
dash.cloudflare.com → My Profile → API Tokens

The init container will create the tunnel and DNS records automatically.

messages.text: Cloudflare Tunnel is running. Wildcard DNS records (*.domain) and ingress rules were created automatically for all DOMAINNAME_N defined in your .env.
To add a new domain: add DOMAINNAME_N to .env, then run: docker compose up -d --force-recreate cloudflare-tunnel-init cloudflare-tunnel
Cloudflare enforces a 100 MB request body limit on proxied traffic. For large file transfers (Nextcloud, Immich) or live video streams (Frigate), use grey-cloud DNS records that bypass the proxy.
To verify: docker logs cloudflare-tunnel-init && docker logs cloudflare-tunnel | grep Registered
